summary: Israel’s air force killed Hezbollah missile commander Mohammed Mousa Mteirek in the Nabatieh area, while Israeli leadership announced strikes on Hezbollah targets in the southern Beirut suburbs. Iran halted indirect message exchanges with the United States and warned that any cease‑fire must include Lebanon, amid escalating rhetoric from Tehran and Israeli officials. US President Trump claimed productive talks with Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu and said no troops would be sent to Beirut.
key_points: Hezbollah missile commander Mohammed Mousa Mteirek was killed by an Israeli Air Force strike in the Nabatieh area, targeting a unit responsible for hundreds of rockets and drones (Source: @manniefabian/58365).

Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu ordered the army to target Hezbollah positions in the southern Beirut suburbs, stating Israel will not allow Hezbollah to target Israeli cities (Source: @VahidOnline/75843).
Iran announced it has halted indirect message exchanges with the United States through mediators in protest over Israeli operations in Lebanon and Gaza (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3228).
US President Donald Trump said he had a productive call with Netanyahu and asserted that no troops would be sent to Beirut, claiming Hezbollah agreed to stop firing (Source: @VahidOnline/75855).
The IDF withdrew its 146th Division from southern Lebanon after three months of operations, leaving only the 91st and 36th divisions in the area (Source: @manniefabian/58360).
Israeli Defense Forces reported a false alarm after alerts for hostile fire from Lebanon toward settlements on the confrontation line (Source: @idf_telegram/24094).
Israeli Home Front Command extended protection time for dozens of northern settlements from 60 to 90 seconds amid heightened alerts (Source: @almogboker78/17273).

sections.executive_summary: Hezbollah missile commander Mohammed Mousa Mteirek was killed by an Israeli air strike in Nabatieh, marking a significant blow to Hezbollah’s rocket capabilities. Israeli leadership announced strikes on Hezbollah targets in southern Beirut and warned of continued operations to protect Israeli civilians. Iran halted indirect talks with the United States, demanding any cease‑fire include Lebanon, while President Trump claimed a productive call with Netanyahu and no troop deployments to Beirut.

points: Iran halted indirect message exchanges with the United States through mediators, citing Israeli operations in Lebanon and Gaza as the cause, and indicated that resistance groups are considering a possible blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and activation of additional fronts such as Bab el‑Mandeb (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3228).

Iranian officials repeatedly emphasized that any cease‑fire between Iran and the United States must encompass all fronts, explicitly mentioning Lebanon as a required component (Source: @VahidOnline/75843).

Iran’s foreign ministry spokesperson warned that continued Israeli attacks on Lebanon would have consequences and linked violations of cease‑fire in Lebanon to violations on all fronts (Source: @VahidOnline/75843).
Iranian media reported that discussions with the United States via mediators have been stopped, potentially leading to escalatory measures in the region (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3228).

Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu ordered the Israeli army to target Hezbollah positions in the southern Beirut suburbs, stating that no situation will allow Hezbollah to target Israeli cities (Source: @VahidOnline/75843).

Israel’s Home Front Command extended the protection time for northern settlements from 60 to 90 seconds, adding specific towns such as Haifa and Kiryat Shmona to the extended alert list (Source: @almogboker78/17273).
The IDF reported a false alarm after alerts for hostile fire from Lebanon toward settlements on the confrontation line, confirming no casualties (Source: @idf_telegram/24094).
Israel’s Defense Minister Yoav Gallant (cited indirectly) reiterated that attacks on Israeli civilians will be met with strikes on terrorist sites in Beirut (Source: @VahidOnline/75843).

US President Trump claimed in a translated statement that he had a productive call with Netanyahu and that no troops would be sent to Beirut, asserting that Hezbollah agreed to halt fire (Source: @VahidOnline/75855).
No explicit operational details about Gaza or the West Bank were present in the source messages.

Hezbollah missile commander Mohammed Mousa Mteirek was killed by an Israeli air strike in the Nabatieh area, a unit responsible for launching rockets and drones at Israeli civilians and troops (Source: @manniefabian/58365).
Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu directed the army to strike Hezbollah targets in the southern Beirut suburbs, emphasizing protection of Israeli cities (Source: @VahidOnline/75843).
The IDF withdrew its 146th Division from southern Lebanon after three months of operations, leaving only the 91st and 36th divisions active (Source: @manniefabian/58360).
Iran warned that continued Israeli attacks on Lebanon could trigger broader regional responses, including potential blockades (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3228).
No explicit operational details about Syria or Iraq were present in the source messages.
No explicit operational details about Gulf states were present in the source messages.

key_developments: Hezbollah missile commander Mohammed Mousa Mteirek was killed by an Israeli Air Force strike in the Nabatieh area, targeting a unit responsible for hundreds of rockets and drones (Source: @manniefabian/58365).

Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu ordered strikes on Hezbollah targets in the southern Beirut suburbs, stating Israel will not allow Hezbollah to target Israeli cities (Source: @VahidOnline/75843).
Iran halted indirect message exchanges with the United States through mediators, protesting Israeli operations in Lebanon and Gaza (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3228).
President Donald Trump claimed a productive call with Netanyahu and asserted that no Israeli troops would be sent to Beirut, with Hezbollah agreeing to stop firing (Source: @VahidOnline/75855).
The IDF withdrew its 146th Division from southern Lebanon after three months, leaving only the 91st and 36th divisions in the area (Source: @manniefabian/58360).
Israel’s Home Front Command extended protection alerts for northern settlements from 60 to 90 seconds amid heightened threat levels (Source: @almogboker78/17273).
Israeli Defense Forces reported a false alarm after alerts for hostile fire from Lebanon toward settlements on the confrontation line, confirming no casualties (Source: @idf_telegram/24094).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah’s missile capabilities have been degraded by the killing of commander Mohammed Mousa Mteirek, yet Israeli officials continue to target Hezbollah positions in southern Beirut, indicating ongoing offensive operations. Iran’s suspension of indirect communications with the United States and its warnings about expanding the conflict to maritime chokepoints suggest a risk of broader regional escalation. US statements from President Trump about diplomatic progress contrast with on‑the‑ground actions, creating uncertainty about the durability of any de‑escalation.
sections.regional_response: Israel is intensifying its military posture in Lebanon, extending civil defense alerts and maintaining a reduced but active division presence. Iran is leveraging diplomatic pressure, demanding that any cease‑fire include Lebanon and hinting at possible naval blockades, while also supporting allied resistance groups. The United States, through President Trump’s remarks, appears to be seeking a diplomatic narrative, though concrete policy shifts remain unclear.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah’s command structure should focus on potential re‑organization after the loss of its missile commander. Iranian communications channels indicate a willingness to open new fronts, requiring vigilance over maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz and Bab el‑Mandeb. Israeli civil defense extensions suggest anticipation of short‑range rocket attacks, warranting continued alertness in northern settlements.
